summary: Two extra experiments aligned with the course title: a LoRA-fine-tuned generative LLM scorer, and a retrieval-augmented (RAG) scorer over a frozen DeBERTa encoder.

experiments.name: qwen2.5_1.5b_lora
experiments.title: Qwen2.5-1.5B-Instruct + LoRA fine-tuning
experiments.headline: JSON-formatted scoring head; generative output post-processed and clamped to {1.0, 1.5, ..., 5.0}.

primary_metric.label: Validation cross-entropy
primary_metric.note: Validation loss reported as NaN under fp16 on Apple Silicon (MPS) when the labels mask is sparse. Training loss converged smoothly: 0.184 -> 0.168 -> 0.165 across 3 epochs.

validation.split: 10% random hold-out from the 3911-essay training set

experiments.prompt_strategy: Single-turn chat: user message asks for the six rubric scores in compact JSON {cohesion, syntax, vocabulary, phraseology, grammar, conventions}. Decoder is post-processed with a regex that extracts the JSON block and snaps each value to the {1.0, 1.5, ..., 5.0} grid.
experiments.kaggle_runtime: Kaggle T4 GPU notebook (offline, internet disabled)
notes: Training was performed locally on an Apple Silicon laptop (MPS, fp16); the resulting LoRA weights are uploaded as a Kaggle Models artifact and consumed by the inference notebook.
Generative output is constrained to the official rubric grid via post-processing rather than a constrained-decoding head.
MCRMSE on the public hidden test set was not measurable client-side because the Kaggle code competition does not expose ground-truth labels.

experiments.name: deberta_v3_base_rag
experiments.title: DeBERTa-v3-base embeddings + cosine-KNN retrieval
experiments.headline: Mean-pooled DeBERTa-v3-base embeddings retrieve the K nearest training essays; their analytic scores are softmax-weighted by similarity.

primary_metric.label: Validation MCRMSE
primary_metric.note: Held-out validation: last 500 training essays, KNN built on the remaining 3411 essays.

config.aggregator: Softmax over similarities (temperature=10) on neighbour analytic scores

experiments.prompt_strategy: Non-parametric scoring: prediction = softmax(sim * 10) * neighbour_scores; no fine-tuning, no decoder.
experiments.kaggle_runtime: Kaggle T4 GPU notebook (offline, internet disabled)
notes: The retriever consumes only the official training corpus; no external knowledge base is required, which keeps the experiment compliant with Kaggle code-competition rules.
Validation MCRMSE 0.5445 lands between the single-alpha Ridge (0.533) and the LightGBM SVD baseline (0.534), and above the stacked classical ensemble (0.519).

Failure mode: when the neighbourhood is dominated by mid-range essays the prediction collapses toward the corpus mean, which mirrors the regression-to-the-mean pattern observed for the classical stack on extreme essays.

comparison_with_classical.decision: Final NTULearn submission keeps the classical stacked_ensemble as the headline entry because (a) it is evaluated under the more rigorous 5-fold stratified CV, (b) it ships as both CSV and Kaggle notebook, and (c) the LLM/RAG variants are reported alongside as evidence that the team explored the course-mandated LLM/RAG axes.
